content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                                    Date : May 6, 1978

                                                                             A-78-05-028

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Gina S-K

Subject: Agenda item at opinion request meeting of April 14, 1978


Is the Berkeley city council making an expenditure when it
spends money advocating a "No" vote on Proposition 13
(Jarvis-Gann)?

Answer: Due to Suit decision (the legislature is not
a person, etc.) we do not believe we may require a city
to file as a committee. Suggested city attorney, district
attorney or attorney general might want to investigate
re misuse of public funds.

State of California

Memorandum

To : Natalie                                    Date : April 14, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Regina

Subject: Item for discussion at the Opinion Request Meeting


Edie Campbell, City Clerk of Berkeley has asked whether or not the
Berkeley City Council which is opposing the Jarvis-Gann Initiative
is required to report city money spent in this effort.

I don't think Berkeley is the only jurisdiction which may be faced
with this question.

sections.question: Is the Berkeley city council making an expenditure when it spends money advocating a "No" vote on Proposition 13 (Jarvis-Gann)?
sections.conclusion: Due to Suitt decision (the legislature is not a person, etc.) we do not believe we may require a city to file as a committee. Suggested city attorney, district attorney or attorney general might want to investigate re misuse of public funds.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Berkeley city council's spending on advocacy against Proposition 13 (Jarvis-Gann) constitutes a reportable expenditure under campaign finance law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded it cannot require a city to file as a committee based on the Suitt decision, but suggested that city, district, or state attorneys may wish to investigate potential misuse of public funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two memoranda discussing the same issue. Second memorandum is partially illegible/degraded. Both address Berkeley's Proposition 13 advocacy spending. Document formatted as internal FPPC memoranda rather than formal opinion letter. Date of April 14, 1978 referenced as meeting date.

Answer: Due to Suit decision (the legislature is not a person, etc.) we do not believe we may require a city to file as a committee. Suggested city attorney, district attorney or attorney general might want to investigate re misuse of public funds. State of California Memorandum To : Natalie Date : April 14, 1978 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Regina Subject: Item for discussion at the Opinion Request Meeting Edie Campbell, City Clerk of Berkeley has asked whether or not the Berkeley City Council which is opposing the Jarvis-Gann Initiative is required to report city money spent in this effort. embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum addressing whether Berkeley city council's expenditures opposing Proposition 13 constitute reportable campaign activity. The FPPC determined it lacks authority to require city filing as a committee under the Suitt precedent but recommended further investigation by other authorities regarding potential misuse of public funds.
